Parent bead ddx-cc298105 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.
- All Go callsites for Store.ReadAll, Store.ReadAllFiltered, Store.Get, and Store.GetWithArchive pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
- Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreReadAll_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreReadAllFiltered_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreGet_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreGetWithArchive_UsesSuppliedContext.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.

3. - `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-cc298105 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.
- All Go callsites for Store.ReadAll, Store.ReadAllFiltered, Store.Get, and Store.GetWithArchive pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
- Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreReadAll_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreReadAllFiltered_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreGet_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreGetWithArchive_UsesSuppliedContext.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.

3. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-cc298105 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.
- All Go callsites for Store.ReadAll, Store.ReadAllFiltered, Store.Get, and Store.GetWithArchive pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
- Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreReadAll_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreReadAllFiltered_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreGet_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreGetWithArchive_UsesSuppliedContext.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.

When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-cc298105 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.
- All Go callsites for Store.ReadAll, Store.ReadAllFiltered, Store.Get, and Store.GetWithArchive pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
- Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreReadAll_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreReadAllFiltered_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreGet_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreGetWithArchive_UsesSuppliedContext.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.

3. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.

Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved.   e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B

  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

NON-SCOPE

When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-63780ef6">     <title>Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="ad3e721ff039bdb81bb9ddfef3f7f5fbdfc12309" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T054324-2fd1d264"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestWorkWatch_SelfRefreshDefaultOn(t *testing.T) {
	factory := NewCommandFactory(t.TempDir())
	factory.Commit = "abc1234"
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("watch", "true"))

	check := factory.buildWorkBinaryRefreshCheck(workCmd, factory.WorkingDir, "")
	require.NotNil(t, check, "watch mode must enable self-refresh by default")
}

func TestWorkWatch_SelfRefreshCanBeDisabled(t *testing.T) {
	factory := NewCommandFactory(t.TempDir())
	factory.Commit = "abc1234"
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("watch", "true"))
	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("self-refresh", "false"))

	check := factory.buildWorkBinaryRefreshCheck(workCmd, factory.WorkingDir, "")
	require.Nil(t, check, "--self-refresh=false must disable the boundary re-exec")
}

func TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged(t *testing.T) {
	factory := NewCommandFactory(t.TempDir())
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("watch", "true"))
	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("project", factory.WorkingDir))
	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("idle-interval", "45s"))
	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("min-power", "40"))
	require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("max-power", "90"))

	exePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "ddx-test-binary")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(exePath, []byte("binary-v1"), 0o755))
	factory.workBinaryRefreshPathOverride = exePath
	factory.Commit = "abc1234"

	var probeCalls int
	factory.workBinaryRefreshProbeOverride = func(ctx context.Context, path string) (string, error) {
		probeCalls++
		require.Equal(t, exePath, path)
		return factory.Commit, nil
	}

	var reexecCalls int
	factory.workBinaryReexecOverride = func(exe string, argv []string, dir string) error {
		reexecCalls++
		return nil
	}

	check := factory.buildWorkBinaryRefreshCheck(workCmd, factory.WorkingDir, "")
	require.NotNil(t, check)

	time.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(exePath, []byte("binary-v2"), 0o755))
	now := time.Now().UTC()
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(exePath, now, now))

	refreshed, err := check(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, refreshed)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, probeCalls)
	assert.Zero(t, reexecCalls, "unchanged installed commit must not trigger re-exec")
}

func TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	projectRoot := minimalProjectDir(t)
	wd, err := os.Getwd()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	runWorkBinaryRefreshGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runWorkBinaryRefreshGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runWorkBinaryRefreshGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	runWorkBinaryRefreshGit(t, projectRoot, "add", ".")
	runWorkBinaryRefreshGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed work refresh repo")

	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	first := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-refresh-0001", Title: "refresh bead 1", Priority: 0}
	second := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-refresh-0002", Title: "refresh bead 2", Priority: 1}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), first))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), second))

	exePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "ddx-refresh-binary")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(exePath, []byte("binary-v1"), 0o755))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
	factory.Commit = "abc1234"
	factory.workBinaryRefreshPathOverride = exePath
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}

	var probeCalls int
	factory.workBinaryRefreshProbeOverride = func(ctx context.Context, path string) (string, error) {
		probeCalls++
		require.Equal(t, exePath, path)
		return "def5678", nil
	}

	var reexecExe string
	var reexecArgv []string
	var reexecDir string
	factory.workBinaryReexecOverride = func(exe string, argv []string, dir string) error {
		reexecExe = exe
		reexecArgv = append([]string(nil), argv...)
		reexecDir = dir
		return nil
	}

	executed := make([]string, 0, 1)
	factory.tryExecutorOverride = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
		executed = append(executed, beadID)
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(exePath, []byte("binary-v2"), 0o755))
		now := time.Now().UTC()
		require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(exePath, now, now))
		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
			BeadID:    beadID,
			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
			ResultRev: "rev-" + beadID,
		}, nil
	})

	out, err := executeCommand(
		factory.NewRootCommand(),
		"work",
		"--watch",
		"--project", projectRoot,
		"--idle-interval", "1s",
		"--min-power", "40",
		"--max-power", "90",
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err, "watch worker must re-exec cleanly when a newer installed binary is detected: %s", out)

	assert.Equal(t, []string{first.ID}, executed, "refresh must fire before the next bead is claimed")
	assert.Equal(t, exePath, reexecExe)
	assert.Equal(t, wd, reexecDir)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, probeCalls)
	require.NotEmpty(t, reexecArgv)
	assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(reexecArgv), 2)
	assert.Equal(t, "ddx", reexecArgv[0])
	assert.Equal(t, "work", reexecArgv[1])
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "--watch")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "--project")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, projectRoot)
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "--idle-interval")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "1s")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "--min-power")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "40")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "--max-power")
	assert.Contains(t, reexecArgv, "90")

	gotFirst, err := store.Get(first.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	gotSecond, err := store.Get(second.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, gotFirst.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotSecond.Status)
}

func runWorkBinaryRefreshGit(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
	cmd.Dir = dir
	cmd.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git %v failed: %s", args, out)
	return string(out)
}
